attacking_style: Opportunistic diesel rider who capitalizes on chaos (echelons, crashes, breaks) with sustained power efforts rather than explosive accelerations. Excels in solo tempo pursuits 8-18km out, timing bridges and counterattacks when others organize.
team_tactics: QuickStep/Soudal-QS deploys him as tactical Swiss army knife: establishes early positioning in wind splits, delivers monstrous leadout pulls through flamme rouge, and launches redundant attacks when teammates are already committed forward.
terrain_preference: Flat-to-rolling Classics terrain with cobbles/wind exposure. Thrives in echelon chaos and sustained diesel efforts on pavé. Vulnerable on uphill sprint finishes (Ninove 2020) where pure power matters more than positioning.
vulnerability: Wet cobbled conditions cause crashes (Roubaix 2021 early, 2022 late from winning position). Fatigue-induced handling errors in race finales. Lacks explosive power to finish uphill sprints despite superior positioning.
key_patterns: Crashes in wet/technical finales when fatigued, twice at Roubaix from winning positions (2021-2022).
Excels at long solo tempo pursuits: velodrome chase 2019, 17km break 2018, echelon counter 2017.
QuickStep uses him for early race wind positioning, securing podium groups before decisive moments.
Delivers leadout/tempo pulls through flamme rouge despite lacking sprint finish (Paris-Nice 2022, Suisse 2018).
Mental resilience: won TT after Roubaix crash, took yellow after spectator incident.
Bridges to lead groups mid-race on hellingen, extending QuickStep's tactical depth beyond climbers.
form_trajectory: neutral: 2023 podium at Brugge-De Panne validates positioning skill, but recent pattern shows late-race handling vulnerability outweighs diesel power gains
